codeReview.summary: The original `shared-consolidate-orchestrator-types` commit did not complete the advertised PlanStatus/CommonTaskStatus alignment, but the blocking code issue is now resolved in the repository. `packages/shared/src/plan.ts` imports `CommonTaskStatus` from `orchestrator-types.ts`, builds `PLAN_STATUSES` from `COMMON_TASK_STATUSES` plus `PLAN_SPECIFIC_STATUSES`, and defines `PlanStatus` as `CommonTaskStatus | PlanSpecificStatus`; the existing plan normalization suite covers the shared-status composition and terminal-status behavior. The fix session itself was handled as a no-op because that alignment had already landed earlier in the branch history.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The current worker guidance does not define how a no-op fix should record provenance when the defect was already resolved by an earlier commit. In this fix session the worker concluded the alignment was already complete, then attached the current HEAD as the feature commit even though that commit was unrelated to the plan/orchestrator files.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for worker session `db0a2067-b030-40f1-8a2c-48c0d62e3d66` shows reads of `packages/shared/src/plan.ts` and `packages/shared/src/orchestrator-types.ts`, the conclusion 'The alignment was already complete - no code changes were needed', then `git rev-parse HEAD` and `EndFeatureRun` with commitId `75fbc10fb4b7340422350bec0709ce7060a2f750`. `git show --stat 75fbc10fb4b7340422350bec0709ce7060a2f750` touches only `tests/unit/suites/profile/*` plus version files, while `git log -- packages/shared/src/plan.ts` shows the actual alignment landed in `c08c3e6` and the import-path follow-up in `7a01fac`.

summary: Pass. The prior blocking PlanStatus/CommonTaskStatus mismatch has been resolved in the codebase, so the original failure condition is no longer present. The explicit fix run was a no-op because the needed alignment had already landed earlier; I recorded a shared-state observation about that provenance gap.
